_description: Structure profiles — intrinsic properties of each structure, independent of market conditions and direction. Direction (call vs put) is resolved at runtime from the sign of (target - forward). Used by the structure scorer to match market state to structure suitability.

_schema.path_dependent: bool — true if a barrier can terminate the trade before expiry
_schema.requires_target: bool — true if the structure needs a specific target level to be properly sized
_schema.overlay_only: bool — can be offered as an overlay/enhancement on a primary position, but should not be the sole expression of a view
_schema.optimal_for: one-line description of the market condition where this structure is the right choice
_schema.major_risk: primary risk that makes this structure a poor choice
_schema.structuring_guidelines: practical guidance on strike selection, premium targets, and construction

vanilla.optimal_for: Full delta participation with no cap; best when vol is low relative to expected move size
vanilla.major_risk: Elevated vol makes premium expensive relative to expected move; theta drag if the move is slow
vanilla.structuring_guidelines: Strike at 25 or lower delta. Interesting if premium is less than 35% of potential profits

1x1_spread.optimal_for: Target level is known; premium saving from sold strike is material; atmfsratio confirms the carry-to-cost is attractive
1x1_spread.major_risk: Spot moves decisively through the sold strike — all further gains are forgone; worst outcome is being right directionally but too conservative on the target

risk_reversal.optimal_for: Zero premium required; skew on the sold side is elevated (making the sold leg expensive) and the PM accepts the downside on the sold leg
risk_reversal.major_risk: Sharp move against the view — sold leg generates continuous loss with no floor; requires margin; inappropriate when the PM has asymmetric conviction (strong upside view but no stomach for downside)
risk_reversal.structuring_guidelines: Standard construction: buy 25Δ in direction of view, sell strike on the other side, targeting zero net premium. Increase leverage by reducing the delta of the strikes

seagull.legs: buy ATM option in direction of view, sell OTM option at target level, sell OTM option on the opposite side to fund
seagull.optimal_for: Near-zero premium with a known target; sold wing on the opposite side is placed at a level the PM considers a credible floor/ceiling with tolerable loss if breached
seagull.major_risk: Spot trades through the sold wing — loss accrues beyond that level with no further hedge; sold option in the direction of the view caps the gain at the target
seagull.structuring_guidelines: Buy ATM forward. Sell in-direction leg at the target level (typically 25Δ–15Δ). Sell opposite-side wing to bring net premium to zero or small credit — wing strike chosen to match the funding need, typically 15Δ–10Δ.

rko.optimal_for: Very high payout ratio when the PM has strong conviction the move will be directional and clean — spot will not trade through the knock-out barrier before reaching the target
rko.major_risk: Spot touches the knock-out barrier at any point during the option life — the trade ceases to exist immediately, regardless of where spot finishes at expiry; path matters more than the final level
rko.structuring_guidelines: Knock-out barrier set beyond the target in the direction of the view. Wider barrier = lower payout ratio but more resilient. Narrower barrier = higher payout ratio but fragile. Cross-check: barrier must be set above a level the market is unlikely to trade through before the target is reached. Payout ratio needs to be higher than 2.25 to be interesting

1x1.5_spread.optimal_for: Reduced-premium spread with moderate tail risk; sits between a 1x1 (fully capped, higher premium) and a 1x2 (near-zero premium, larger tail); PM accepts a net short 0.5 position beyond the target in exchange for a lower upfront cost
1x1.5_spread.major_risk: Spot moves well through the target — net short 0.5 position beyond target generates losses that increase with spot; less severe than a 1x2 but still open-ended; confirm PM understands the residual short above target
1x1.5_spread.structuring_guidelines: Buy 1 option at K1 (ATMF or ½σ toward target). Sell 1.5 options at the target level. Net premium lower than a 1x1 spread but not fully zero. Max profit at target. Beyond target the position is net short 0.5 options — smaller tail than a 1x2 but still present. Suitable when the PM wants meaningful cost reduction without fully committing to the 1x2 tail risk profile.

1x2_spread.optimal_for: Near-zero premium with a known target; PM accepts being net short beyond the target level in exchange for paying no premium
1x2_spread.major_risk: Spot moves well through the target — the two short legs leave a net short position above the target, generating losses that increase with spot; unlimited risk beyond target
1x2_spread.structuring_guidelines: Buy 1 ATM forward option. Sell 2 options at the target level, sized so net premium ≈ 0. Max profit realised at the target. Beyond target the position is net short one option — confirm the PM understands and accepts this. Target should be a high-conviction level, not a vague estimate. Check that the short gamma above target is tolerable given portfolio context.

european_digital.optimal_for: Fixed known payout if spot finishes through the strike at expiry; useful as a comparison to quantify the binary value of a threshold being breached
european_digital.major_risk: Binary gamma near expiry makes the position extremely sensitive to small moves at the strike; all-or-nothing payout is difficult to manage into expiry; overpriced vs vanilla for large moves
european_digital.structuring_guidelines: Strike set at the target level or at a key threshold (e.g. a round number or technical level). Payout fixed in USD notional terms. Premium typically lower than vanilla at same strike but payout is fixed — compare premium vs expected payout to assess EV. Looking for sub 13% premium to be interesting.

european_digital_rko.optimal_for: Lowest-premium binary expression; PM has strong conviction that spot will not trade through the knock-out barrier during the life and will finish through the digital strike at expiry
european_digital_rko.major_risk: Two independent ways to lose: spot touches the knock-out barrier at any point (trade dies immediately), or spot finishes on the wrong side of the digital strike at expiry; the combined barrier and binary risk makes this the most fragile structure
european_digital_rko.structuring_guidelines: Digital strike at the target level or key threshold. Knock-out barrier set beyond the target in the direction of the view — same logic as a standalone RKO. Premium materially lower than a standalone digital at the same strike. Use as a pricing reference to illustrate the cost of stacking barrier and binary risk; rarely appropriate as a primary trade.
